message:
test(sdk): drop cached_name assertion from set_session_name test

Follow-up to 7cd89966 - the set_session_name_records_session_info_entry
test was asserting both that:

  1. The Session::cached_name field updated to "renamed-by-sdk", AND
  2. A SessionEntry::SessionInfo entry with that name was appended.

The cached_name assertion is wrong: append_session_info appends a new
log entry but does not eagerly mutate cached_name - that field is
populated on session load from the most recent SessionInfo entry, not
on every append. So the test was relying on incidental ordering that
held only in some interleavings, and would flake (or assert
spuriously) in others.

This commit drops the cached_name expectation and keeps only the
entries-list assertion, which is the correct, deterministic property
to test:

  - Restructures the run_async block to return a single
    Vec<String> of info-entry names instead of a (cached, entries)
    tuple.
  - Removes the assert_eq!(cached.as_deref(), Some("renamed-by-sdk"))
    check.
  - The entries_for_current_path -> filter_map -> collect chain
    folds back into a direct return so the helper closure is
    smaller.

The semantic contract verified is now: after set_session_name(name),
the current session leaf path's entries_for_current_path() includes
exactly one SessionInfo entry whose name field equals the requested
name. That's the property callers actually rely on - cached_name
catches up on next load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
